CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, data VARBINARY);
SELECT isbinary(data) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT isbinary(data) AS is_varbinary FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
SELECT (CASE WHEN isbinary(data) = 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END) AS varbinary_check FROM test_table;
SELECT id, isbinary(data) FROM (SELECT * FROM test_table) AS subquery;
SELECT t1.id, isbinary(t2.data) FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE isbinary(data) = 1 UNION SELECT * FROM another_table WHERE isbinary(info) = 1;
SELECT isbinary(0x12345abc) AS binary_test, isbinary(123) AS non_binary_test;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data) SELECT 3, info FROM another_table;
SELECT isbinary(data) FROM (SELECT id, data FROM test_table WHERE id IN (1, 2)) AS filtered_data;
